title: hieracium lachenalii subsp. subirriguifrons
intro: Hieracium lachenalii subsp. subirriguifrons, also known as the Alpine Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The plant is thought to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Hieracium lachenalii subsp. subirriguifrons can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
